description_long: This compact cabin features a rectangular structure with a flat, slightly sloped roof and is clad in what appears to be oriented strand board (OSB) or plywood panels, giving it a utilitarian, unfinished look. The front facade includes a single door and a double-pane window, while the side and rear elevations show additional small windows, one of which is paired with an external air conditioning unit. The cabin's exterior is marked by visible framing and insulation panels, suggesting a focus on basic thermal efficiency. There are no decorative elements, porches, or overhangs, and the overall design is highly functional and minimal. The windows are asymmetrically distributed, with varying sizes and placements, and the door is positioned on the front face, adjacent to the largest window. The structure appears suitable for temporary or semi-permanent urban placement, such as in parks, lots, or as an accessory dwelling unit.
description: A small, boxy cabin with a flat roof, OSB panel cladding, and asymmetrically placed windows and a single front door. The design is minimal and utilitarian, with visible framing and no decorative features.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance door, a large double-pane window, and part of the interior.
description_view_1: Side view with a single small window and a security camera mounted nearby.
description_view_2: Plain side view with no windows or doors, only visible paneling and framing.
description_view_3: Rear view featuring a small window with an external air conditioning unit installed below it.
attribute_roof_type: flat roof with a slight slope, constructed from OSB panels
attribute_window_arrangement: Three windows of varying sizes: a large double-pane window on the front, a small single window on the left side, and a small window with an air conditioning unit on the rear. The arrangement is asymmetrical and irregular.
attribute_door_placement: One exterior door located on the front facade, positioned to the left of the large window.
